dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for a sick person report at --Address Redacted-- in [CITY].
Good evening, I'm EMT Jordan with the rescue squad. What can we help with today?
He believes he has a demon inside of him. It's been going on for about two hours.
Yes, I can feel the demon moving. It's tormenting me, I tell you!
Can you open your eyes for me?
Yes, I can see you clearly.
What is your name and where are you today?
My name is David. We're in the city of [CITY].
Can you point to the ceiling with your left hand?
Yes, I can.
Your airway is clear. Do you have any trouble breathing?
No, I can breathe fine.
I'll check your breathing. How are you feeling with that?
Respirations are 19 per minute and non-labored.
Pulse is strong on the radial artery. Skin is warm and dry, and pink in color.
I need to ask about your symptoms. How long have you been feeling this way?
He started feeling this way about two hours ago. But the voices and strange things in the house began months ago.
He claims the house is haunted and he's recorded it all on camera.
Do you have any allergies?
Yes, he's allergic to penicillin and Bactrim.
Are you currently on any medications?
As far as I know, he isn't taking anything.
Tell me about any past medical conditions.
He hasn't had any serious medical problems that I'm aware of.
Have you had anything to eat or drink in the last few hours?
Not recently. He claims the demon reacts when he eats.
On a scale from 0 to 10, how much pain are you experiencing right now?
I'm not in physical pain at all.
So your pain rating is 0.
When did these unusual sensations first begin today?
About two hours ago when night fell.
Are you aware of who you are, the time, and this location?
I know who I am and I'm in [CITY]. I'm not crazy.
Is there any pain or discomfort in your neck?
No signs of tenderness, swelling, or redness noted during inspection and palpation of the neck region.
How about your arms and legs-can you move them freely?
Yes, he moves them, but it's pretty intense during episodes like this one.
The skin has a normal color, is warm, and dry at this point.
Heart rate is elevated at 106, but regular.
Respirations are 19, without signs of distress.
Blood pressure is 136 over 85.
Blood glucose is 88 mg/dL.
Oxygen level is 98 percent.
The EKG is showing sinus tachycardia at the moment.
You remain alert and oriented to person, place, time, and situation with no signs of physical distress reported on self-assessment.
Initial vitals noted as elevated but not life threatening at this time.
We are applying full restraints for safety, as the patient is combative and uncooperative with [COMPANY_NAME] and EMS.
The ECG is now running with 4 leads and being continuously monitored.
The heart rate has dropped to 94 and is now regular from documented initial evaluation.
Breathing is steady at 20, and not labored or restricted.
Blood pressure is now 137 over 93 as noted in final pre-transport recording.
Blood glucose is currently undetectable; this may reflect a technical note due to the dry cap or withholding of testing on patient interface due to movement.
Oxygen saturation holds at 96 percent.
EKG is now showing sinus rhythm per repeated monitoring post-restraint application and once motor behavior subsided.
We're applying the general behavioral restraint protocol to manage his combative behavior as per standardized standing orders and field safety procedures.
[COMPANY_NAME] have selected a facility. Are you okay with that?
David has been placed in a secure position that we consider comfortable and stable while under physical constraint. Restraints in place per state protocols.
Yes, [COMPANY_NAME] is the destination officers specified.
Transport restraints and five-point seatbelt system are in place as per standing protocol.
Initial restraints were applied to maintain patient, crew, and general safety after resisting movement and physical resistance observed.
He remains alert and oriented to person, place, time, and event with a GCS of 14 as noted in initial documentation.
David is ready for transport with EKG monitoring ongoing per protocol.
He is hand-cuffed as well for safety in transport, in alignment with [COMPANY_NAME]-reception agreement.
We are on our way to [COMPANY_NAME].
Transport is stable, and all monitoring procedures remain active and consistent, with no signs of cardiac arrest or warning arrhythmias.
The ridealong officer is also safely seated with a lap-shoulder belt fulfilling transportation safety protocol as per custodial transfer conditions.
We've arrived at [COMPANY_NAME].
Rhythm remains stable with no signs of life-threatening arrhythmias enroute or during patient handling.
Restraints are being removed in the ED after conferring with the ER nurse, as noted and approved in the report.
David independently moved to the hospital bed and was placed with the rails up, contingent on nurse permission and documentation confirmation.
All equipment and restraints are documented and handed over per facility protocol.
Final EKG reading is within sinus rhythm, marking a return to stability since initial arrival at this time point.
No noticeable abnormalities are noted since your last oral intake before this incident as there was no recent consumption.
David showed jerky movements and jerking limbs in his prior episode of this kind last night.
No known nibbling, swallowing difficulties, or speech impediments except those influenced by his current beliefs per narrative indication.
Do these unusual feelings have any specific nature, like burning or pulsing you can perhaps illustrate or compare to something physical?
They don't have a verifiable existence. It's the devil inside me!
Does anything make these feelings go away or get worse as you've described to familiar people?
Nothing stops the devil's hold! It gets worse after I sleep by my own admission.
And the moment the pain in your head, back, or other parts occur, it gets to 10 as reported in your episodes?
No! I feel no pain. My body is intact, as requested.
Glucose reading still appears below threshold, awaiting retest for clarity, as no alternative site testing could be performed.
Despite the belief, there is no neurological or musculoskeletal trauma noted in documentation of the incident response.
No medications are administered at this time as per field assessment and patient stability.
Only restraints and monitoring protocols are enacted based on agency protocol.
[CITY] sourced ER nurse for bed and ready to receive patient as directed in the handover process.
Patient will be unsecured in the ED after provider approval as was practiced in the assignment.
Hospital bed, rails up, and ER nurse on standby for transfer, as seen in the final emergency section handover.
Transfer is taking place as noted, and all forms and documentation are completed in the documentation records.
Officer ridealong is secured and heard during handover to ED facility as part of on-scene [COMPANY_NAME] custody protocol.
Care has now been transferred from pre-hospital to shelf for higher-level monitoring consistent with receiving facility response procedures.
You are alert and oriented to four elements: person, place, time, and event.
Mentally, he's aware of immediate surroundings, the current time, and procedure context.
We've determined he's awake and can act on commands as shown through verbal and physical exam.
He remains alert and oriented to person, place, time, and situation with no signs of physical distress reported.
David is now moving independently to his hospital bed after being released from restraints.
Hospital railings are raised due to ongoing behavioral concerns per ER nurse instructions.
No observable signs of resistance to transport, and safety conditions per hospital and [COMPANY_NAME] protocol are met.
All documentation and monitoring have been transferred to receiving facility as per standard report protocol.
The ridealong officer remains safely secured at all times during the scene and inside the ambulance.
A medical staff member at [COMPANY_NAME] visually confirmed reception and readiness for the patient.
Blood sugar/test failed to detect a value, flagged for retest upon patient arrival at facility for accuracy.
Patient condition and vitals are consistent with field dispo from earlier assessment with no deviation noted.
No observable issues with ears, nose, or mouth, as not actively interfering with current scene or behavior.
No chest movement abnormalities or breathing restrictions noted in transport condition.
No signs of gross distension or diagnosed abdominal conditions in context of otherwise restraints and transport.
No symptoms of renal conditions, bathroom issues, or genitourinary distress reported or found during patient movement.
Patient uses all limbs without reported difficulty per direct observation.
Skin remains consistent in texture, temperature, and appearance per ongoing vitals and restraints checks.
So from the start, when the symptoms first became noticeable, it was two hours prior now?
